tests.title: Mock Test 1 — NTPC Pattern
tests.description: 25 questions covering Maths, Reasoning and General Awareness in roughly NTPC proportions. A starter test — more will be added over time.

questions.subject: Math
questions.question: Find the HCF of 36 and 60.
questions.explanation: 36 = 2²×3², 60 = 2²×3×5. Common factors: 2²×3 = 12.

questions.subject: Math
questions.question: A man buys an article for ₹500 and sells it for ₹575. Find his profit percentage.

questions.subject: Math
questions.question: Simplify: 15% of 250 + 20% of 150.

questions.subject: Math
questions.question: Find the simple interest on ₹2000 at 5% per annum for 3 years.

questions.subject: Math
questions.question: Two numbers are in the ratio 3:5 and their sum is 64. Find the smaller number.
questions.explanation: 3x+5x=64, x=8. Smaller number = 3×8 = 24.

questions.subject: Math
questions.question: A train 150 m long crosses a pole in 10 seconds. Find its speed in km/h.

questions.subject: Math
questions.question: Find the next number in the series: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ?
questions.explanation: Differences are 4,6,8,10,12 — so next term = 30+12 = 42.

questions.subject: Math
questions.question: The average of 5 numbers is 18. If one number is excluded, the average becomes 16. Find the excluded number.
questions.explanation: Sum of 5 = 90, sum of remaining 4 = 64. Excluded number = 90-64 = 26.

questions.subject: Reasoning
questions.question: Find the odd one out: Triangle, Square, Circle, Wood.
questions.explanation: The first three are geometric shapes; 'Wood' is a material, not a shape.

questions.subject: Reasoning
questions.question: If A=1, B=2, ... Z=26, find the sum of values of the letters in 'CAT'.

questions.subject: Reasoning
questions.question: Complete the analogy: Doctor : Hospital :: Teacher : ?
questions.explanation: A doctor works at a hospital; a teacher works at a school.

questions.subject: Reasoning
questions.question: Pointing to a photo, Ravi said, 'She is the daughter of my grandfather's only son.' If Ravi's grandfather has only one son, how is the girl related to Ravi?
questions.explanation: Grandfather's only son is Ravi's father, so the girl is Ravi's father's daughter — his sister.

questions.subject: Reasoning
questions.question: In a code, 'RIVER' is written as 'SJWFS'. How would 'WATER' be written in the same code?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by 1: W→X, A→B, T→U, E→F, R→S, giving XBUFS.

questions.subject: Reasoning
questions.question: Statements: All cats are animals. Some animals are dogs. Conclusion: Some cats are dogs. Does the conclusion follow?
questions.explanation: The statements don't establish any direct link between cats and dogs, so the conclusion does not necessarily follow.

questions.subject: Reasoning
questions.question: Find the missing number: 4, 9, 16, 25, ?, 49
questions.explanation: These are squares of 2,3,4,5,6,7. The missing term is 6² = 36.

questions.subject: Reasoning
questions.question: A man walks 4 km North, then 3 km East. How far is he from his starting point?
questions.explanation: By Pythagoras theorem: √(4²+3²) = √25 = 5 km.

questions.subject: GA
questions.question: Who is the Chief Justice of India as of mid-2026?
questions.explanation: Surya Kant was sworn in as the 53rd Chief Justice of India on 24 November 2025.

questions.subject: GA
questions.question: The Ram Mandir at Ayodhya was consecrated (Pran Pratishtha) in January of which year?
questions.explanation: The consecration ceremony was held on 22 January 2024.

questions.subject: GA
questions.question: Which Indian athlete won India's first individual Olympic gold medal in athletics, at the Tokyo 2020 Olympics?
questions.explanation: Neeraj Chopra won gold in the javelin throw at the Tokyo 2020 Olympics (held in 2021).

questions.subject: GA
questions.question: How many houses does the Indian Parliament have?
questions.explanation: Parliament has two houses: the Lok Sabha (House of the People) and the Rajya Sabha (Council of States).

questions.subject: GA
questions.question: Which Indian city hosted the G20 Summit in 2023?
questions.explanation: India hosted the G20 Summit in New Delhi in September 2023.

questions.subject: GA
questions.question: Where is the headquarters of the World Health Organization (WHO) located?
questions.explanation: WHO is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.

questions.subject: GA
questions.question: Who took charge as Governor of the Reserve Bank of India in December 2024?
questions.explanation: Sanjay Malhotra was inaugurated as the 26th RBI Governor on 11 December 2024.

questions.subject: GA
questions.question: From which date was the Goods and Services Tax (GST) implemented in India?
questions.explanation: GST was rolled out nationwide on 1 July 2017.

questions.subject: GA
questions.question: India won the 2024 ICC Men's T20 World Cup by defeating which team in the final?
questions.explanation: India beat South Africa in the final on 29 June 2024.

tests.title: Mock Test 2 — Group D Pattern
tests.description: 25 questions covering Maths, Reasoning, General Science and Current Affairs in roughly Group D (Level 1) proportions.

questions.subject: Math
questions.question: Find the LCM of 12 and 18.

questions.subject: Math
questions.question: A shopkeeper marks an item at ₹800 and gives a 25% discount. Find the selling price.

questions.subject: Math
questions.question: Find the compound interest on ₹10,000 at 10% per annum for 2 years.

questions.subject: Math
questions.question: A can finish a work in 12 days and B in 18 days. Working together, how many days will they take?
questions.explanation: Combined rate = 1/12+1/18 = 5/36 per day, so time = 36/5 = 7.2 days.

questions.subject: Math
questions.question: A boat's speed in still water is 12 km/h and the current's speed is 4 km/h. Find the time to travel 32 km downstream.
questions.explanation: Downstream speed = 12+4 = 16 km/h. Time = 32/16 = 2 hours.

questions.subject: Math
questions.question: Find the area of a circle with radius 7 cm (use π = 22/7).

questions.subject: Math
questions.question: Find the next term: 3, 7, 15, 31, 63, ?
questions.explanation: Each term follows ×2+1: 63×2+1 = 127.

questions.subject: Reasoning
questions.question: Find the odd one out: 121, 144, 169, 200
questions.explanation: 121=11², 144=12², 169=13² are perfect squares; 200 is not.

questions.subject: Reasoning
questions.question: If P means '+', Q means '−', R means '×', S means '÷', find the value of: 10 R 2 P 6 S 3

questions.subject: Reasoning
questions.question: Complete the series: A, C, F, J, ?
questions.explanation: The gap increases by 1 each time: A+2=C, C+3=F, F+4=J, J+5=O.

questions.subject: Reasoning
questions.question: Find the odd one out: Mumbai, Delhi, Kolkata, Sahara
questions.explanation: The first three are cities; Sahara is a desert.

questions.subject: Reasoning
questions.question: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
questions.explanation: 'The only daughter of my mother' is the woman herself, so the man's mother is the woman — she is his mother.

questions.subject: Reasoning
questions.question: Statements: All pens are pencils. All pencils are erasers. Conclusion: All pens are erasers. Does it follow?
questions.explanation: Since all pens are pencils and all pencils are erasers, all pens must be erasers — the conclusion logically follows.

questions.subject: Reasoning
questions.question: A is facing North. He turns 90° clockwise, then 180° more. Which direction does he now face?
questions.explanation: 90° clockwise from North = East. A further 180° turn from East = West.

questions.subject: Science
questions.question: The SI unit of electric current is:
questions.explanation: Electric current is measured in Amperes (A).

questions.subject: Science
questions.question: Which gas is most abundant in Earth's atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of the atmosphere by volume.

questions.subject: Science
questions.question: The 'powerhouse of the cell' is the:
questions.explanation: Mitochondria generate the cell's energy (ATP), earning the nickname 'powerhouse of the cell'.

questions.subject: Science
questions.question: The pH value of a neutral solution is:
questions.explanation: A pH of 7 is neutral; below 7 is acidic, above 7 is basic.

questions.subject: Science
questions.question: Deficiency of which vitamin causes scurvy?
questions.explanation: Vitamin C deficiency causes scurvy.

questions.subject: Science
questions.question: Sound cannot travel through:
questions.explanation: Sound is a mechanical wave and needs a medium — it cannot travel through a vacuum.

questions.subject: GA
questions.question: India's 'Operation Sindoor' (May 2025) was launched in response to which terrorist attack?
questions.explanation: Operation Sindoor followed the April 2025 terror attack on tourists in Pahalgam, Jammu & Kashmir.

questions.subject: GA
questions.question: Who took oath as Bihar's Chief Minister for a record 10th time after the November 2025 assembly election?
questions.explanation: Nitish Kumar took oath as Bihar CM for a record 10th time on 20 November 2025 after the NDA's landslide win.

questions.subject: GA
questions.question: Chandrayaan-3 made its successful soft landing near which region of the Moon?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-3 landed near the Moon's south pole on 23 August 2023 — now marked as National Space Day.

questions.subject: GA
questions.question: In which year did India win the Women's Cricket World Cup for the first time?
questions.explanation: India won its first-ever Women's Cricket World Cup title on 2 November 2025.
